entry.title: Re: supplier invoice gets posted without accountant release
entry.content:
Hello,

it sounds as if the workflow is not set correctly. 

Have you check the workflow (Fiori-App: Manage Workflows for Supplier Invoices) ?

There you should check whether the following setting is active in the invoice approval workflow:

"Automatically post after approval" → This must be deactivated.

An additional step "Back to accounting" should be added after authorization.

Distinction between "Done" and "Post" for the accountant

If the accountant does not post an invoice, but only marks it as "completed", then only the approval workflow may be triggered.

Recommended: Accountant should either do nothing or have a workflow step that marks "For review", not "Completed".

Define workflow steps with "Return to previous processor"

Workflow can be configured so that after approval by the buyer, the document is not posted but sent back to the accountant's inbox.

 

Best regards

Dennis

entry.title: Re: Batch managing in SAP B1
entry.content:
Hi @MarinaLevy ,

When on production order, you first need to issue the components. SAP will prompt the batch selection window.

Then, you receipt the final product. Here, you are creating the batch for the finished goods.

Finally, you can run a report to track the batches assigned to the Production Order

If you're not able to perform any of these steps, please provide details. I hope it helps.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Transportation chain determination in Vendor Master
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a query related to Transportation chain determination in Vendor Master. In most of the Vendor Master (Control Data) the Transportation chain field is showing only 2-3 records in dropdown list. But there are some in which the dropdown list shows all the available transportation chain in it. We are using this in SAP ECC in Retail business processes. 

Any information like how it is fetched in Vendor master and how it can be limited will be helpful. 

Thank you all.

entry.title: Re: supplier invoice gets posted without accountant release
entry.content:

Thank you for the reply, as have following setting, if could not find any other setting in manage supplier invoice workflow. that would be great see any documents or steps that can be implemented .

Kindly suggest any links or documentation .

entry.title: Re: Data schedule in SAP DataSphere using Cron expression
entry.content:
Your current cron logic is like:

  • It's one of days 1-20 of the month, OR
  • It's a weekday (Monday through Friday)

Correct solution could be:
46 3,11 1-20 * */1

entry.title: Re: How to Read Data Stored in SAP Datasphere from SAP BW Bridge

 A database error occured. The error message in the database was: |
| "Unable to connect remote source: [SAP AG][LIBODBCHDB SO][HDBODBC] |
| Communication link failure;-10709 Connection failed (RTE:[200117] |
| Failed to initiate any authentication method.

entry.title: Re: Error connecting SAP Datasphere to SAP Commerce Cloud via Odata - Error
entry.content:
It seems Your configuration or source is incorrect.
Can you provide more details about source settings / oData You try to connect with? It need to be correct oData service (v2 or v4)

entry.title: Re: Need to change the company code currency (300 FI docu...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

Recap of the current situation

You've got EUR set as the company code currency, and more than 300 FI documents have already been posted. Now the client wants to switch the currency to MAD - totally understandable if their operations are shifting focus, but unfortunately, it's not that simple.

So here’s a direct answers to your different questions :

Can you technically change the company code currency?

Short answer: no, not once transactions have been posted.
The local currency is deeply embedded in how SAP stores financial data. It’s tied into everything — journal entries, balances, asset values, controlling, reporting, you name it. Changing it after the fact would break way too many things.

Even SAP doesn’t offer a standard way to do this in a live system. It’s not supported.

 

Is it a good idea to try anyway?

Honestly, no.
Even if you could somehow force the change using backend tools or by working with SAP directly, it would be extremely risky. You’d be looking at potential data inconsistencies, reporting errors, and possibly compliance issues. Not to mention the huge amount of effort it would take to clean everything up afterward.

 

What should you do instead?

The best and cleanest solution is to create a new company code with MAD as the currency. That way:

  • You keep your existing data intact
  • You can start fresh with the correct currency
  • No risk of breaking existing postings or reports

If needed, you can migrate open items and balances from the old company code to the new one, either manually or using SAP’s migration tools. And if both company codes will still be used, you can handle transactions between them using intercompany functionality.

 

I hope those elements will help you with your current problems.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: How to properly Bind SAPUI5 SmartTable in CAP to SAP...
entry.content:
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/31996305/bind-smarttable-with-entityset-of-an-entity/32012301#32012301

entry.title: Re: Usage Statistics of Table and Canvas Reports to Plan Transition to Stories

entry.title: Re: Can I extend a certain app? With Clean Core? How?
entry.content:
Dear mlauber,

Thanks for sharing your details on this very important topic. I tried to extend a standard Fiori App, called F2023. The end-user just wants the scheduled start and scheduled end date from the maintenance order to be available as filter and column.

 

First of all the while tracing I came to know that CDS C_Malfunctionreport is used for the list output. However this one cannot be extended via Cloud Development, only Key User extensibility (which is no option due to its limitations if I'm right).

A view extension like below will provide the required functionality (refer to above screenshot):

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZXMALREPO'

@EndUserText.label: 'Extension for C_Malfunctionreport'

extend view C_Malfunctionreport with ZX_C_Malfunctionreport

{

I_MaintNotificationTechObj._MaintenanceOrder.ScheduledBasicEndDate,

I_MaintNotificationTechObj._MaintenanceOrder.ScheduledBasicStartDate

}

 

However it gives 3 ATC errors (which is expectable due to the contracts):

  • Usage of not released application API. The use of CDS Entity I_MAINTENANCEORDER is not permitted
  • Usage of not released application API. The use of CDS Entity I_MAINTNOTIFICATIONTECHOBJ is not permitted.
  • Usage of not released application API. The use of CDS Entity C_MALFUNCTIONREPORT is not permitted.

How would you proceed to go ahead with a clean core approach? Thanks in advance for sharing your inputs.

 

Regards, Bart

entry.title: Re: Question regarding Deep Links for List View in SAP Se...
entry.content:
Hi @Karthik_Murugan 

In SAP Service Cloud Version 2, deep linking to list views like the Case object (/go/list/case) is supported — but filtering the list view via URL parameters is currently not officially documented or supported by SAP.

If this use case is critical, one option is to build a lightweight extension app on SAP BTP.

I hope it helps.

entry.title: Re: Generic Delta Extraction using Function Module not wo...
entry.content: Hi Alex, Table / Data Source need to have defined Primary Key (not the filed with that name, but field(s) combination that will specify key. If it's not defined You have to use Initial Load or use Data Flows.

Your production order must be released and you have to right-click and select "Issue Components". Report completion follows same steps.

entry.title: Re: Create a Simple IDoc Interface with AIF Configuration

entry.title: Variant Configuration use planned / production order quantity for constraints
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I'm working with SAP LO-VC and variant routings. I want to influence the generated routing steps based on the planned or production order quantity. For example:

  • If the order quantity is 50 or less, the routing should include steps 1 through 5.

  • If the quantity is more than 50, steps 2 and 3 should be replaced with an alternative step 2a.

However, during routing explosion, I can't find any way to access the actual order quantity in the dependency logic. I’ve tried looking for system variables or internal parameters, but none seem to be available at that point in the process.

So my questions are:

  1. Is there any standard way to access the planned or production order quantity during variant routing determination?

  2. What is the best workaround to use the order quantity in dependency logic (e.g. by transferring it into a characteristic before routing is exploded)?

  3. Is it even technically possible to conditionally select operations in the routing based on the order quantity?

Any help, tips, or example implementations would be highly appreciated.
Thanks in advance!

I have reDefining a standard OData Service for add some standard and custom field, but after activations and /generation the label for Filter group was disappears .

I have reDefining a standard OData Service without problem, except label for Filter group.

i don't understand the problem , someone can help me?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: New company code as a copy of an old one
entry.content:
Consider standard transaction, EC01, to make the copy, where almost all settings, but not everything, will be transferred to the new company code.

  • Add a SAP tag code for the SAP version, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud, for better answers.
  • Don't forget to watch the support for any notes about Bulgaria changeover to Euro

entry.title: Enhancing productivity and reducing mental load: AI Agents
entry.content:
Enhancing Productivity in Business Life

In the professional realm, AI agents can be invaluable allies. They can manage emails, schedule meetings, and prioritize tasks, ensuring that one can focus on strategic decision-making rather than administrative duties. Virtual assistants can help streamline project management and team collaboration. In our SAP software, the Joule Agent Builder can be a helpful resource to build your own Agent that supports you in your daily work.

Moreover, AI agents can also assist with networking by suggesting relevant contacts and opportunities, and even drafting personalized messages. For people in leadership roles, AI agents can analyse team performance, identify areas for improvement, and provide actionable feedback.

Reducing Mental Load in Personal Life

AI agents can also be a game-changer in personal life. Imagine having a virtual assistant that can manage your private calendar, remind you of important events, and even help with meal planning or the organization of your toddler’s birthday party. AI agents can handle routine tasks such as grocery shopping, sending reminders for appointments, and organizing family schedules. They can also provide personalized recommendations for leisure activities, helping to find time to relax and recharge.

For parents, AI agents can assist with organizing children's activities, homework deadlines, and even suggest educational resources. Health-focused AI applications can monitor fitness goals, provide dietary advice, and offer mental health support through guided meditation and stress-relief exercises. By automating these tasks, AI agents free up mental space, allowing to focus on what truly matters.

Creating Your Own AI Agent in Copilot Chat

But let’s talk practical: creating an AI agent tailored to your needs is easier than you might think. Here’s a step-by-step guide to get started:

  1. Define the Purpose: Determine what tasks your AI agent will perform. Will it manage your calendar, provide health advice, or assist with business analytics?
  2. Gather Data: Collect and preprocess the data your agent will need. This could include text, images, or other relevant data types.
  3. Choose Algorithms: Select the appropriate machine learning algorithms and frameworks, such as TensorFlow or PyTorch, to train your agent. You may want to use the resources on open.hpi.de to learn more about those tools.
  4. If needed: Implement NLP: If your agent needs to understand and generate human language, incorporate natural language processing (NLP) techniques.
  5. Integrate with UI: Connect your trained model with a user interface, such as a chatbot platform or a mobile app, to make it accessible.
  6. Monitor and Update: Continuously monitor your AI agent’s performance and update it to improve its capabilities and adapt to new tasks.

entry.title: Re: How to Read Data Stored in SAP Datasphere from SAP BW Bridge
entry.content: @pankaj_singh21: Could you please create a support case for your issue? This will give us the option to logon to your tenant, analyze the root cause, and find a solution.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere CDS View live modelling
entry.content: My suggestion would be to implement a two-tier approach: replicate standard CDS views via Replication Flows (supporting both initial load and delta synchronization) followed by creating semantic View wrappers on top of this imported raw data. This architecture balances near real-time data currency with optimized query performance while circumventing the annotation constraints of direct remote table consumption. The View wrapper layer also provides an abstraction that shields downstream models from potential structural changes in the source system.

entry.title: Restrict Batch Entry in Subcontracting GR (543) and Return (542) to Match Exact 541 Batch
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In our subcontracting process, we often send the same material under multiple batches (e.g., Batch A and Batch B) using multiple 541 movements for the same PO and same material.

However, during goods receipt (543) or component return (542), the system currently allows users to enter any of those batches, even if it doesn’t match the batch that was actually sent in the corresponding 541 document for that quantity.

This leads to issues like:

  • Incorrect batch reconciliation

  • Vendor stock mismatch

  • Incorrect consumption reporting

💡 Requirement:

We want the system to:

  • Allow only the exact batch (from 541 movement) for which the quantity is being received or returned.

  • If a user tries to post a GR or return using a different batch, even from the same PO and material, the system should throw an error and block the posting.

  • This should apply for MIGO and MB1B, for 543 and 542 movement types respectively.

Has anyone implemented such a batch-level validation in subcontracting scenarios?
Are there any standard options, BAdIs, or enhancement points you would recommend to achieve this?

Appreciate your guidance!

Shlomi

Meanwhile, I also made the field invisible and then visible again, and made a test print of both cases, to confirm that the field is responsive to changes. Confirming that it is.

entry.title: Re: Automatically Closing of credit desicions in SAP
entry.content:
Hi Franziska,

Thank you for bringing up this important topic.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance, when credit decisions are automatically closed by the "Workflow User", it typically indicates that a workflow rule or condition within the Manage Workflows for Credit Memo Requests or Sales Document Workflows has been met—triggering an auto-approval or rejection based on system logic rather than manual intervention.

Key Areas to Investigate:
1. Workflow Conditions in Productive System
Check the configuration in the app:
App: Manage Workflows for Credit Memo Requests
→ Review the conditions that may lead to auto-closure (e.g., value thresholds, risk class, credit exposure).

2. Business Rules (BRF+) and Automatic Credit Check Settings
Navigate to:
App: Manage Your Solution → Configure Your Solution
→ SSCUI for Credit Management: Define Automatic Credit Control

This section determines how the system automatically handles credit limit checks, including triggers for auto-decision logic.

3. "Workflow User" Behaviour
In Cloud environments, the Workflow User is a technical user who executes a task when a decision step is auto-resolved. This may occur due to,

  • A default rule without agent assignment
  • A timeout configuration with a default outcome
  • A fallback decision path in the workflow

4. Audit Logs and Process Visibility
You can use:

  • App: Workflow Instances (F2081)
  • App: My Inbox – Completed Tasks
    → These allow you to trace how and why the decision was executed, including the responsible agent 

Setting up Configuration for Workflows

Let me know if you need further support

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: What is difference between Forward Propagation and HR...
entry.content:
Hello,

HRIS Sync is the process that syncs data from Employee Central out to the User Data File (UDF), also called the Basic file when implementing EC. The data elements inside EC are hard coded to specific fields on the UDF or you can set up others to be mapped to the available fields within the UDF, such as the Custom fields. You can learn more about this important process in this KBA (https://me.sap.com/notes/2172427/E) or in the Employee Central Implementation guide.

Forward Propagation refers to the update of data inside of Employee Central, particularly in Job Information or Compensation Information. If you are updating data in these sections via the UI or Import, the data will be carried forward (propagated) to subsequent records. A good example would be updating an employee's Job Information by inserting a new record and only changing the Work Schedule. All of the other values would be propagated from the previous values, unless you have business rules in place to make changes if the Work Schedule is updated. These might be the Standard Weekly Hours or FTE fields. You can find additional information in the EC Guide here: https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-employee-central/implementing-employee-central-core/forward-propagation-in-job-information?q=propagation

entry.title: Re: LMS user license type update automation
entry.content:
Hello there,

Depending on the scenario of how you import the users, but technically we would use the User Connector - SF for platform users, which should have a status of "Active", and use the User Connector for external learners, which should have a status of "Functional".

By separating the connectors, you should be able to predefine the license type from the config file for the connectors.

Hope that helps.

Thanks, Sunny

Best regards, 
Costiantyn

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere External Access Overview: APIs, CLI and SQL
entry.content:
Hey Henri, great work putting all this info together!

This is super helpful for navigating the different ways to access Datasphere externally. 

One thing I noticed though - we're still missing proper APIs to fully interact with DSP programmatically. The CLI is useful but limited, and each approach (REST, OData, SQL) only gives you part of what you need depending on what you're trying to do.

Would be nice to have a more unified API layer someday rather than this fragmented approach. But your guide definitely makes working with what we have much easier! Thanks for documenting all this in one place.

entry.title: A quick introduction to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Diagnostic Tool
entry.content:
What is the SAP SuccessFactors Diagnostics Tool?

The Employee Central Diagnostic Tool is a self-service feature that helps HR Administrators, Implementation Consultants and Customer Support teams identify and resolve system issues quickly. Whether it's a failed workflow, a misfiring rule, or a configuration inconsistency, the tool offers visibility into what's happening behind the scenes - without needing to comb through backend logs.

Key Benefits

  • Workflow & Rule Tracing: Understand exactly which rules and workflows triggered during transactions like New Hire or ESS/MSS events.
  • Centralized Services Transactions (Employee Data Save Transactions): Trace how data changes are processed and spot issues in real-time. And how save rules are executed in detail and sequence.
  • Proactive Troubleshooting: Catch misconfigurations early and reduce dependency on external support.

How It Works

This tool is built leveraging the Centralized Services Feature which was incrementally delivered for all EC entities in last 3 years.

When Should You Use It?

Use the Diagnostic Tool when:

  • A workflow fails to trigger, or routes incorrectly.
  • Business rules don't behave as expected during a hire, transfer, or data change.
  • You want to validate configuration changes in a test environment.
  • You're preparing logs and insights for a support ticket.
  • You need to know what the reason for a field value change was when you actually changed another field.
  • You want to proactively audit rule execution and system behavior.

Want to try it?

  1. Head to Admin Center > Diagnostic Tool in your SAP SuccessFactors environment, choose the type of transaction you with to analyze (e.g. Centralized Services or Workflows).

     

  2. Enable tracing using the link in landing page if you want to capture save transactions. For workflows trace capturing this enablement is not required.

     

  3. Next, reproduce the issue by saving changes in Employee's Data- any HRIS/EC entity like Job, personal, compensation information with right Role-based permissions enabled for you.
  4. Go back to the Diagnostic Tool and search for your recent transaction by entering 'Requested By' and today's date.
  5. Select the transaction you want to analyze and review Snapshot view for field changes or Rule Execution View to analyze rule executions during the transaction.

     

Final Thoughts

This tool puts diagnostic power directly in your hands - making it easier to resolve issues faster, ensure compliance, and improve user experience.

Resources:

Here are Official SAP Resources:

SAP Help Portal - Diagnostic Tool

What's New Viewer - 1H 2024 - New Diagnostic Tool for Employee Central Transactions

What's New Viewer - 2H 2024 - New View and Enhancements to the Diagnostic Tool

What's New Viewer - 1H 2025 - Enhancements to the Diagnostic Tool

Knowledge Base Articles:

 3468733 - Diagnostic Tool for Employee Central save transactions and workflows

3469437 - Incorrect email notification, workflow approvers, business rule for workflow or workflow configuration is selected by system - Employee Central
3469432 - Centralized Services save actions are resulting in incorrect data update - Employee Central

Next Steps:

Looking forward for you to use this tool and try if it helps in your configurations/issue analysis, share your feedback how useful it was for you, and also if anything else you would like to see in this tool with me!

You can leverage the comment section here or formally create SAP Customer Influence idea.

2090228 - How to submit enhancement ideas for SAP SuccessFactors Products

entry.title: Re: Hybris user not authenticated after SAML successful r...
entry.content:
@phoude 

I am facing the same issue as you did. Implementing CDC as IDP for B2B.

entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content:
@HarveyXu ...it's less of a "need" and more of a desire to not disrupt the status quo.  For reasons I am not privy to, that's how it's been at our organization: BizX has historically sorted users as FIRST M LAST, and Learning has sorted users as LAST, FIRST M.  

The inability to turn off General Display Name for 1H 2025 disrupts that.  It's not as if that poses major problems, but it is an annoyance that Learning administrators have been vocal about, so...an easy way to manipulate sort order in both environments independently (without having to license Employee Central) would be a "nice to have."

entry.title: Re: S4Cloud: Adobe Form - custom field hardcoded text not...
entry.content: Hi Katja, maybe that link will help you. It is in german but you can translate it via google. It always helped me when it comes to javascript development in adobe forms:https://www.berater-wiki.de/JavaScript_und_FormCalc_Scripting_im_LiveCycle_Designer

entry.title: EC benefits UK pension to Infotype 0071
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Has anyone implemented the integration of EC Global Benefits for UK pensions to Infotype 0071 in ECP?
Our ECP consultant has suggested using a custom MDF portlet for pensions, integrated via SFAPI. However, the customer prefers to maintain a standard EC setup using Global Benefits.

We are also recommending an integration approach using CPI to transfer data from EC Global Benefits to Infotype 0071 in ECP.

Would appreciate your thoughts—what would be the best approach in this scenario?

entry.title: Re: New company code as a copy of an old one
entry.content: In a sandbox system you could set the client witch SCC4. In a quality system, import from development.

entry.title: Re: SAC – Integrated Financial Planning – DRIVER6 & alloc...
entry.content:
Hi again, @Susanne_Helbig .

We are running the public cloud version in the backend. The documentation you sent is for the private edition. However, I took a look at the equivalent of that documentation for the public cloud version. As you can see, the procedure is quite different. Furthermore, I believe we have successfully implemented the required steps in this documentation. This is because, without a functioning communication scenario, system, agreement etc. we would not be able to import any data or perform any queries. In our system, we are able to import data from the public cloud edition system we are running, so I believe these steps have been performed correctly.

For the public cloud edition, the documentation states that the folder Statistical Key Figures – FINSSKF should be visible, but it is not. Note, please, that many other folders are visible and queries can be built from them and imports can be executed.

Is it possible that there is a different underlying issue?

Thank you again for your help and best regards!

entry.title: Re: SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 7: UI Design and Technology for Web and Mobile
entry.content:
Are the custom space templates and custom page templates available on Fiori Launchpad 2025 On-Premise?

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/working-with-space-templates-uist

At the top of the documentation is says "cross-product", but further down: "Launchpad Page templates can be created for SAP BTP ABAP Environment and SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP Environment."

entry.title: Re: How to properly Bind SAPUI5 SmartTable in CAP to SAP...
entry.content: google please, the question is answered before.....

entry.title: Re: Issue with Determining Current Day of the Week in CDS...
entry.content:
Hello Saikrishna,

As I can see that you're currently using the following logic:

MOD(DATS_DAYS_BETWEEN(CAST(CONCAT(SUBSTRING($session.system_date, 1, 4), '0101') AS ABAP.DATS), $session.system_date), 7) + 1 

This return incorrect results because January 1st of a year doesn’t always fall on a Monday, and you're using that as the reference "day 1 


Let's consider known reference date (e.g., 01.01.1900, which is a Monday), rather than the start of the current year:

Calculation logic should be

MOD(DATS_DAYS_BETWEEN('19000101', $session.system_date), 7) + 1

This assumes:

  • 1900-01-01 was a Monday (which it was),

  • The calculation returns days of the week as: Monday = 1, ..., Sunday = 7. 

For example, let's say today's date is 2025-04-22 (Tuesday): 

DATS_DAYS_BETWEEN('19000101', '20250422') = 45751
MOD(45751, 7) + 1 = 2  → Tuesday

Best Regards,

Pooja

entry.title: Re: S4Cloud: Adobe Form - custom field hardcoded text not...
entry.content: Thanks for pointing that out Junwu. You are right, I have very limited or basic programming experience and hardly any formal training in it, so pardon my vocabulary. I'm learning by doing. So far, I have managed to get most of our output management changes up and running, but this one I can't figure out. I'm not sure it relates to the javarscript or if it could be some other type of issue.

entry.title: Re: S4Cloud: Adobe Form - custom field hardcoded text not...
entry.content: Thanks Fabienne, much appreciated!

entry.title: Re: FieldGlass integration for requisition creation
entry.content:
Hi Arjen

A prerequisite to Position Management/Job Requisition integration is an exclusive SSO configuration for the integration user between Success Factors and Fieldglass. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-employee-central/using-sap-fieldglass-with-employee-central/prerequisites-for-employee-central?locale=en-US

Part of this configuration in Fieldglass must be done by a Fieldglass representative and you'll need to raise a ticket. You need to provide Success Factors' OData API url, username, password and API version.

BR,

Josie

entry.title: Re: RAP Application with two Root View Entity with Associ...
entry.content: got your struggle! If you’re avoiding composition, the fix for unmanaged is all about letting the parent handle the child's create, not the child itself. You can’t define create in the item’s behavior. it’ll always throw that "dependent entity" error. Instead, in the header’s behavior, enable create via its association to the item (like association _Items { create; }). For managed, composition automates child CRUD, just define the parent-child as a composition and skip manual logic. Both work, but unmanaged needs manual parent-controlled creation. Check associations and parent behavior setup

entry.title: Re: How to properly Bind SAPUI5 SmartTable in CAP to SAP...
entry.content: Alright.

entry.title: Re: S4Cloud: Adobe Form - custom field hardcoded text not...
entry.content: post your latest code, let's see what is going on.

entry.title: Re: Using a Function Module in SAP RAP managed
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have solved the issue by wrapping BAPI_USER_CREATE1 logic in a remote-enabled custom function module

I have created a custom function module with the below parameter but first make sure to enable remote module as shown below

 

Next for the import i used the below ofc you can add more attributes if you want to use more just use BAPI_USER_CREATE1 as your reference. 

As for the source code i used the below

and at last i called the custom FM in my method as shown below

METHOD add_user.
  READ ENTITIES OF zi_user1_rh IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY User
    ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    RESULT DATA(users).

  LOOP AT users INTO DATA(user).
    CALL FUNCTION 'Z_CREATE_SU01_USER'
      STARTING NEW TASK 'USRCREATE'
      DESTINATION IN GROUP DEFAULT
      EXPORTING
        iv_username  = user-username
        iv_firstname = user-firstname
        iv_lastname  = user-lastname
        iv_password  = 'passworD123@@'.
  ENDLOOP.

    READ ENTITIES OF zi_user1_rh IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY User
    ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    RESULT DATA(userlist).
    result = VALUE #( FOR user1 IN userlist ( %tky = user-%tky %param = user  ) ).


  ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: How to assign role validity dates different from user access dates (WS USER_ACCESS_REQUEST)
entry.content:
HELLO,

it's my first question, enjoy

We have an Identity Management application wich call webservice USER_ACCESS_REQUEST on SAP GRC system, to create/modify users in a S/4HANA productive system, with ARQ and BRM GRC' modules.

Is it possible to manage different validity date scenarios for user access and role validation ?

apparently the sap standard only calculates dates for roles; if roles have a time limit, then validity dates at account level will be adjusted, 
validfrom and validto dates are not taken into account in the userinfo parameter group.

For example, how do you manage access for a student or trainee who is only present for a month in the future? e.g. between September and December 2025?

with other value or other parameter ? now with ReqType (002) and ProvAction (006)

thanks a lot for your support !

David

entry.title: Re: Fetching Change request number with customer number o...
entry.content:
Hi,

It has been a long time ago where I was developing for the MDG... I rembered tables starting with USMD ... maybe that helps? I think there is also a class ... cl_usmd_* and the method read_entity_data or something like that. Maybe you can check this out.

entry.title: Re: Using a Function Module in SAP RAP managed
entry.content:
Hi,
The error occurs because the RAP framework manages its own database commits. When you call BAPI_USER_CREATE1 directly within a determination or action, the BAPI’s internal COMMIT WORK conflicts with the framework’s implicit commit handling, causing a nested commit error. Here’s the solution:

Call BAPI_USER_CREATE1 via RFC FM in a separate LUW:

CALL FUNCTION 'ZCreateUser'
      STARTING NEW TASK 'UPDATE'
      DESTINATION 'NONE'
....

This would definitely resolve the dump you are getting.
RAP the Bapi in an RFC FM.

entry.title: Re: Message no. SO322; document of this type is not allowed in your system
entry.content:
The procedure to solve the error is described in the SAP note 2184005 - Error message: Documents of this type are not allowed in your system (SO322) for attachments - SAP for Me

1) Transaction SOPE. Check that all the document types are there and configured correctly.

2) Transaction SLG1 of object SCMS (KPro) may show authorization errors or other errors:

3) Report RSCMST to check the server

entry.title: Re: Renewed Server Certificate - SQLA Client fails with "The TLS handshake failed, error code 0
entry.content: Does web client logging reveal more details, say a particular (smaller) OS error number ?

entry.title: FU: No propagation of dates and times between stage 1 and 2
entry.content:
Hello,

For SAP TM, we are currently working with support package SAPK-16010INSAPTM.

We are experiencing an issue with scheduling from the Transport Cockpit.
We have FUs with three stages:
- Stage 1: visible in the TC
- Stage 2: not visible in the TC
- Stage 3: visible in the TC

Stage 2 of these FUs is not visible in the TC because no date (neither pickup date nor delivery date) is initially entered.

This wouldn't be too serious if the date/time propagation worked when scheduling stage 1: the Arrival date/time from stage 1 would then be propagated to stage 2 as the Earliest Acceptable Pick-Up Date/Time... and the second stage of the FU would then be visible in the TC.

However, this propagation mechanism works well for FUs of at most two stages... but fails for FUs of at least three stages.

How can this problem be solved?

Thank you for your support

Kind regards,

entry.title: Re: Direct Deployment of UI Integration Cards to SAP Work Zone
entry.content:
Regarding the deployment of the UI integration cards to the BTP subaccount: is this limited to Cloud Foundry / HTML5 apps repository?

Or can UI Integration cards also be deployed to BTP ABAP and subsequently provided to Work Zone (standard or advanced) via the BTP ABAP environment as a content provider, just like other custom BTP ABAP Fiori apps?

entry.title: Re: Using a Function Module in SAP RAP managed

entry.title: Re: Unexpected Credit Check Error During PGI Without an a...
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, even if a Credit Segment is not explicitly assigned to a Sales Area, the system can still perform a credit check during Post Goods Issue (PGI) if the Business Partner maintains an active Credit Segment at the master data level.

Secondly, you’re encountering a credit check failure during Post Goods Issue (PGI) even though no Credit Segment is maintained for the Sales Area. According to standard O2C/OTC behaviour, this should suppress the credit check; however, it still gets triggered in your scenario.

Verified Root Cause (S/4HANA Public Cloud)

Credit checks are still triggered if

1. A Credit Segment Exists at Business Partner Level
Even if the Sales Area is not assigned, an active Credit Segment (e.g. 1010) for the Business Partner activates credit management globally.

This is a standard behaviour in the Public Cloud environment and is not over-ridden by the absence of Sales Area mapping.

2. PGI Triggers a Scope-of-Check That Is Credit-Sensitive
PGI (Post Goods Issue) uses a delivery document type that may include a scope of check, including credit control.

This check is unconditionally triggered if credit management is active globally (via the BP’s Credit Segment).

3. Cross-Sales Area Credit Logic Applies
Even if the specific Sales Area doesn’t have a mapping, the system inherits behaviour from other Sales Areas or Channels where a Credit Segment exists.

Solution: Ensure Full Consistency Between Credit Segment and Sales Area

Option 1: If You Want to Disable Credit Checks
Open the “Manage Business Partner” app.

Navigate to the Credit Segment Data tab.

Remove or deactivate the Credit Segment (e.g. 1010) for that Business Partner.

Ensure no other credit segments exist under that BP that could trigger global credit management.

Option 2: If Credit Management Is Required – Assign Sales Area Properly
Open the “Assign Sales Area to Credit Segment” configuration app.

Add entries to map the relevant Sales Areas to the Credit Segment (e.g. 1010).

This prevents “orphan” credit control behaviour and gives you clean control over credit checks by Sales Area.

Option 3: Adjust or Review PGI Scope-of-Check
PGI errors might also stem from the scope of the check defined in the system for outbound delivery types.

S/4HANA Public Cloud, this can only be changed via SSCUI (if exposed) or SAP Expert Configuration via an incident.

Feel free to let me know if you need further assistance

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Post Goods receipt for service order
entry.content:
Hi SAP experts,

To post a goods issue to an order in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as a Warehouse Clerk, you'll typically follow a process that involves using the "Post Goods Movement" app.

However, this does NOT work with service orders -> Error message below.

Is there a way how this can be configured? I would like to have an app for the warehouse clerk to post a goods issue for a service order.
BR
Simon

Order 8000000080 not found or not permitted for goods movement

Message no. C6001

Diagnosis

Either the order 8000000080 does not exist or you have specified an order for which the function you want cannot be executed. For example, no goods receipt for a CO internal order or no goods issue with reference to a reservation can be carried out.

Procedure

Check your entries and select an order for which the desired function is permitted.

entry.title: Express document "Update was canceled" received from author in FB05
entry.content:
I received the error "Express document 'Update was canceled' received from author" while trying to clear a document in FB05.
When I checked SM13, I found the update termination (screenshot attached).
I also encountered the same issue when attempting to reverse the document in FB08.
I also checked the number range, but it didn’t help.

Has anyone faced this before? Any suggestions on how to resolve this?
Thanks in advance for your support!

entry.title: Search for equipments with characteristic values "greater than" XYZ in CL30N
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am facing the problem, that I have a Characteristic with class XYZ and classtype 002 and cannot add an other comparison operator.

I added a characteristic in the date format. The field for allowing intervals is checked.

Now when i search for equipments in the CL30N (classtype 002), I can use "inclusive" and "exclusive" only.

Is there a way to allow "greater than" and "less than"?

Thank you in advise!

entry.title: Re: SAC - BW live connection - dynamically activate Bex c...
entry.content:
Hello,

a bit late, but there you go:

//get a list of BEx Conditions
var xCondis = Table_1.getDataSource().getBackendConditions();
//get a condition with its ID:
var xCondi = Table_1.getDataSource().getBackendCondition(ConditionID);
//check if it's active:
xCondis[0].getSelected();
//activate / deactivate
xCondis[0].setSelected(true/false);

 Hope it helps others!

entry.title: Re: Restrict Batch Entry in Subcontracting GR (543) and R...
entry.content: Try the solution given here  for the same requirement.

entry.title: Re: Adapt a Maintenance Notification Type for Use in 9-Phase Maintenance Process of SAP S/4HANA Main
entry.content: Hello all, is this blog and some of the content contained in it slightly out of date now? I noticed a note (3571459) that suggest it may be possible to provide the ability to create Maintenance Requests via the same App F1511A by using Adapt UI to include the Notification Type in a dropdown? Has anyone come across this?

entry.title: Re: Outbound process with SAP EWM: POSC, WOCR & Wave Management
entry.content:
Hi Sai Kumar,

While the internal process steps in SAP EWM are predefined and somewhat hardcoded, it is indeed possible to customize them through development if there are specific business requirements. However, in most cases, the standard internal process steps provided by SAP are comprehensive and robust enough to cover a wide range of warehouse scenarios effectively, minimizing the need for custom enhancements.

Thank you!

entry.title: SAP IAS Federated Access AWS Cognito
entry.content:
Hello SAP-Community,

In a recent project for a client, we had a feature request that consisted of making a Fiori application available in SAP BTP Workzone, so far so good. Currently, this client has a mobile application developed by a third party that uses AWS Cognito as an identity provider (IdP) and our need is to provide access to this Fiori application without a request new login.

My question would be whether it is possible to establish a trust relationship between SAP IAS and AWS Cognito so that a new login operation is not required? If so, is there any documentation or a step-by-step guide to help us?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Want to merge transaction line in SAC model based on a dimension member
entry.content:
Hello,

I am using SAC planning model and have data like this:

 

DIM ADIM BDIM CAmount
01/04/2025APEQMP155
01/04/2025APEQMP255
01/04/2025APEQMP355
02/04/2025APEQMP170
02/04/2025APEQMP270
02/04/2025APEQMP370

I want data to be like this:

 

DIM ADIM BDIM CAmount
01/04/2025APEQMP55
02/04/2025APEQMP70

So basically, i want to merge my DIM C lines to 1 line and put the average of the amount against it. And later want to delete those 3 lines.

Please not that i already have master data updated for DIM C.

 

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Create and Update not appearing in Fiori Elements App in CAPM

entry.title: Re: S4Cloud: Adobe Form - custom field hardcoded text not...
entry.content: Do you mean all code from the whole output form template, or just from this field? The full code is obviously long and could contain sensitivities. Here below is all code from that particular field (with the customer code partly censored). Most of it is copied from another field, but the field name, the caption and the piece of javascript are specific to this field. </field> <field name="CustomerVATRegistrationCH" w="150mm" h="6.15mm"> <ui> <textEdit> <border presence="hidden"/> <margin/> </textEdit> </ui> <font size="9pt" typeface="Calibri"/> <margin topInset="1mm" bottomInset="1mm" leftInset="1mm" rightInset="1mm"/> <para vAlign="middle"/> <caption reserve="60mm"> <para vAlign="middle" spaceAbove="0pt" spaceBelow="0pt" textIndent="0pt" marginLeft="0pt" marginRight="0pt"/> <value> <text xliff:rid="A047A429-4532-493D-8F02-BB7E1BC7EFB9">VAT No.CH:</text> </value> <font size="9pt" typeface="Calibri" weight="bold" baselineShift="0pt"/> </caption> <border> <edge presence="hidden"/> <corner/> <corner presence="hidden"/> <corner/> <corner presence="hidden"/> </border> <bind match="none"/> <event activity="initialize" name="event__initialize"> &lt;script contentType="application/x-javascript">if(this.parent.Partner.rawValue =="100XXX") {this.rawValue ="TEST";} else { this.presence = "inactive"; } &lt;/script&gt; </event> <value> <text maxChars="20"/> </value> </field>
